reasoning: The resolution criteria require strictly more than 10 'Riot' events to occur in Papua New Guinea within a 30-day period. The baseline value of 1.000 indicates that over the past 360 days, exactly zero ACLED-recorded 'Riot' events occurred in the country. A jump from an average of zero per month to 11+ in a single month would require a widespread, sustained nationwide crisis significantly larger or more protracted than even the Jan 2024 riots (which were concentrated in a few cities over a couple of days). There are currently no ongoing strikes, military mutinies, or protests reported in November 2025 that could serve as a trigger for such an explosion of violence in the next 7 days or next month. For the distant date (Feb 2026), there's marginally more time for an unanticipated political or economic crisis to develop, but 11+ events remains an extremely high bar in a 30-day window for a dataset that has recorded zero such events over the prior year.
